chunks.section_title: Flex Q4 and Fiscal Year 2022 Headline Results
chunks.content:
Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) reported results for its fourth quarter and fiscal year ended March 31, 2022, announced May 4, 2022.

Fourth Quarter Fiscal Year 2022 Highlights:
- Net Sales: $6.9 billion
- GAAP Operating Income: $228 million
- Adjusted Operating Income: $295 million
- GAAP Net Income Attributable to Flex Ltd.: $168 million
- Adjusted Net Income: $244 million
- GAAP Earnings Per Share: $0.36
- Adjusted Earnings Per Share: $0.52

Fiscal Year 2022 Results of Operations:
- Net Sales: $26.0 billion
- GAAP Operating Income: $972 million
- Adjusted Operating Income: $1,169 million
- GAAP Net Income attributable to Flex Ltd.: $936 million
- Adjusted Net Income: $945 million
- GAAP Earnings Per Share: $1.94
- Adjusted Earnings Per Share: $1.96

CEO Quote: "Flex achieved record performance this year, reflective of the team's incredible execution and dedication to deliver for our customers and all our stakeholders," said Revathi Advaithi, CEO of Flex. "We have strong momentum as we head into fiscal year 2023 and we remain focused on driving sustainable growth, margin improvement, and creating shareholder value."

metadata.section_title: Flex Q4 and Fiscal Year 2022 Headline Results

chunks.section_title: First Quarter Fiscal 2023 and Full Year Fiscal 2023 Guidance
chunks.content:
First Quarter Fiscal 2023 Guidance:
- Revenue: $6.6 billion to $7.0 billion
- GAAP Operating Income: $235 million to $265 million
- Adjusted Operating Income: $285 million to $315 million
- GAAP EPS: $0.33 to $0.39
- Adjusted EPS: $0.44 to $0.50, which excludes $0.06 for stock-based compensation expense, $0.04 for net intangible amortization, and $0.01 for Nextracker LLC series A redeemable preferred units dividends payable in kind.

Fiscal Year 2023 Guidance:
- Revenue: $27.7 billion to $28.7 billion
- GAAP EPS: $1.63 to $1.78
- Adjusted EPS: $2.09 to $2.24, which excludes $0.26 for stock-based compensation expense, $0.15 for net intangible amortization, and $0.05 for Nextracker LLC series A redeemable preferred units dividends payable in kind.

metadata.section_title: First Quarter Fiscal 2023 and Full Year Fiscal 2023 Guidance

chunks.section_title: Schedule V — Notes to Schedules I, II, and III (Non-GAAP Definitions and Footnotes)
chunks.content:
FLEX AND SUBSIDIARIES — NOTES TO SCHEDULES I, II, and III (Schedule V)

(1) Non-GAAP Financial Measures: Flex discloses non-GAAP financial measures including non-GAAP operating income, non-GAAP net income, and non-GAAP net income per diluted share. These supplemental measures exclude restructuring charges, customer-related asset impairments (recoveries), stock-based compensation expense, intangible amortization, other discrete events as applicable, and related tax effects.

Adjustment definitions:
- Stock-based compensation expense: Non-cash charges for estimated fair value of stock options and unvested restricted share unit awards granted to employees and assumed in business acquisitions. Excluded to provide more accurate comparisons to peer companies.
- Intangible amortization: Primarily non-cash charges impacted by timing and magnitude of acquisitions. Excluded when evaluating ongoing performance and forecasting earnings trends.
- Restructuring charges: Include severance for rationalization at existing sites and corporate SG&A functions, asset impairment, and charges related to closures and consolidations of operating sites. Not directly related to ongoing or core business results. During the three and twelve-month periods ended March 31, 2021, the Company recognized approximately $26 million and $101 million of restructuring charges respectively, most of which related to employee severance.
- Legal and other: Costs not directly related to core business results including commercial disputes, government regulatory and compliance, intellectual property, antitrust, tax, employment or shareholder issues, product liability claims, acquisition-related costs, and customer-related asset recoveries. During Q4 FY2022, the Company accrued for certain loss contingencies and recorded an approximately $13 million gain upon successful settlement of certain supplier claims.
- Interest and other, net: Various items not directly related to ongoing or core business results, such as gains or losses related to divestitures, currency translation reserve write-offs, debt extinguishment costs, and impairment charges or gains associated with certain non-core investments. In September 2021, the Company received approval from Brazilian tax authorities for a Credit Habilitation request and recorded a total gain of 809.6 million Brazilian reals (approximately USD $149.3 million as of October 1, 2021) under other charges (income), net. In FY2022, the Company recognized approximately $32 million equity in earnings from value increases in certain non-core investment funds. In FY2021, the Company recognized realized gains of approximately $45 million from a distribution by a non-core investment fund, offset by a $35 million impairment charge.
- Adjustment for taxes: Tax effects of various adjustments incorporated into non-GAAP measures and certain adjustments related to non-recurring settlements of tax contingencies. During FY2022, the Company recorded a $18.6 million benefit for the release of valuation allowances on certain deferred tax assets due to its acquisition of the Anord Mardix business, offset by $13 million tax expense related to the sale of 500,000 redeemable preferred units (Series A Preferred Units) of Nextracker LLC to TPG Rise Flash, L.P.

(2) Beginning in Q2 FY2022, the Company elected to include operating income as a subtotal in the condensed consolidated statements of operations. Deferred revenue and customer working capital advances were separately presented in current liabilities. Certain unbilled receivables previously in accounts receivable are now presented as contract assets. The Company reclassified $146.8 million of unbilled receivables from accounts receivable to contract assets for the period ended March 31, 2021. These presentation changes had no impact on results of operations or cash flows.

(3) In Q4 FY2022, the Company sold 500,000 Series A Preferred Units to TPG Rise, representing a 16.7% interest in its subsidiary, Nextracker LLC, for $487.5 million net of issuance costs. The Series A Preferred Units are redeemable upon the occurrence of conditions not solely within the control of the Company; accordingly, the Company classified the redeemable noncontrolling interest as temporary equity on its consolidated balance sheet. The Company recorded two months of dividend payable in kind totaling $4.2 million as income available to redeemable noncontrolling interest, which is excluded from non-GAAP measures.

metadata.section_title: Schedule V — Notes to Schedules I, II, and III (Non-GAAP Definitions and Footnotes)
